english: serve up for (clerical persons) Dzl., gsól-lo (gsol lo) mčód-čig (mchod cig) I place it before you, help yourself! moreover: rgyál-pos (rgyal pos) čáb-la (chab la) sku-krús (sku khrus) gsol (gsol) the king took a bath Glr.; srás-la (sras la) sku-krús (sku khrus) gsol-lo (gsol lo) they administered a bath to the prince Glr.; mtsan (mtshan) gsol-ba (gsol ba) to assume, to receive, a name Glr., to give a name Glr.; even thus: dei (de'i) tŭgs-la (thugs la) gdon (gdon) gsol (gsol) a demon enters his (the king's) body (clothes himself with it) Glr. Comp. *sol-kár* (*sol kar*) W., resp. for kar-yol (kar yol) earthen ware, crockery. — gsol-skrúm (gsol skrum) meat prepared for the table of a man of rank. gsol-ká (gsol ka) 1. request, prayer Sch. 2. meat and drink Sch. — *sól-kaṅ* (*sol kang*) resp. for *yó-kaṅ* (*yo kang*) C., *tab-tsáṅ* (*thab tshang*) W. kitchen. — gsol-ṅán (gsol ngan) poisoned food Glr. — gsol-lcòg (gsol lcog) table. — gsol-mčód (gsol mchod) prayer and offerings. — gsol-já (gsol ja) tea. — gsol-nyá (gsol nya) fish destined or dressed for the table of a respected person, rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i) Pth. — gsol-tág (gsol thag), gsol-stégs (gsol stegs) table. — gsol-tíb (gsol tib) tea-pot. — gsol-táb (gsol thab) fire-place, kitchen. — gsol-ldóṅ (gsol ldong) = gur-gúr (gur gur). — gsol-dpon (gsol dpon) prop. head-cook, master-cook, gen. cup-bearer, butler, waiter. — gsol-pógs (gsol phogs) salary Sch. — *gsoi-biṅ* (*gso'i bing*) C. = gsol-tíb (gsol tib). — gsol-már (gsol mar) butter. — gsol-tsígs (gsol tshigs) dinner. — gsol-zíb (gsol zhib) fine parched barley-flour. gsol-gyóg (gsol g.yog) under-waiter, under-butler. — gsol-rás (gsol ras) distribution of victuals, by a person of rank to common people, hence Chr. P. for Lord's supper, holy communion; donation, gift, present in gen. II. sbst. 1. request, demand, entreaty, gsol-ba (gsol ba) ₒdebs-pa ('debs pa) to make a request, to entreat frq. — 2. food *sól-wa żé-pa* (*sol wa zhe pa*) to eat, dine, sup C.

v. gsó-ba (gso ba), gsos byéd-pa (gsos byed pa) to cure Sch.; bu mi gsos-pa (bu mi gsos pa) not keeping, retaining a child alive Do.; gsós-bu (gsos bu) foster-son, adoptive son; gsós-ma (gsos ma) cure? medicine? gsós-ma (gsos ma) ₒdébs-pa ('debs pa) Thgy.
resp. for zan (zan) food, fare Glr.; *bsaṅ-ma* (*bsang ma*) id. W.; bsaṅ-ₒbrás (bsang 'bras) resp. a dish of rice.

thought, thinking, bsám-gyis mi kyáb-pa (bsam gyis mi khyab pa) beyond the reach of human intellect, incomprehensible etc. frq.; čos (chos) bzáṅ-las (bzang las) bsam (bsam) bzaṅ (bzang) lhág-pa (lhag pa) yin (yin) a good way of thinking is worth more than good (external) religion Mil.; W.: *sam-ṅan-čan* (*sam ngan can*) malicious, wicked.

I. fut. tense and secondary form of sém-pa (sem pa). II. sbst. सङ्कल्प, 1. thought, imagination, fancy, bsám-pa ṅán-pa sém-pa (bsam pa ngan pa sem pa) to foster bad thoughts Do.; bsám-pa tsám-gyis (bsam pa tsam gyis) quick as thought; rtág-tu ₒdi snyám-du bsám-pa skyes (rtag tu 'di snyam du bsam pa skyes) he was constantly haunted by these thoughts Dzl. — 2. will, mi żig-gi bsám-pa ltar (mi zhig gi bsam pa ltar) (or bżin-du) (bzhin du) sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa) to execute, to carry out a person's will frq.; bsám-pa ltar ma gyúr-pas (bsam pa ltar ma gyur pas) as it did not go according to their wish; bsam-pa ₒdi-las ma zlog čig (bsam pa 'di las ma zlog cig) do not try to divert me from my purpose Dzl.; bsám-pa bzáṅ-po (bsam pa bzang po) good intention or design Mil.; desire, mind, inclination, liking, gnód-pai (gnod pa'i) or gdúg-pai (gdug pa'i) thirst for blood, murderous disposition Glr. — 3. soul, heart (of rare occurrence), bsám-pai dón mi grub (bsam pa'i don mi grub) then (by doing so) you injure your own soul Mil. Comp. bsam-gtán (bsam gtan), ध्यान, 1. state of complete abstraction, acc. to Burn. 'contemplation' (cf. tiṅ-ṅe-ₒdzin (ting nge 'dzin), समाधि, समापत्ति, Burn. 'méditation'), bsam-gtán byéd-pa (bsam gtan byed pa) to transpose one's self into the state of contemplation or meditation (the difference between the two is not easily defined), v. Köpp. I, 586. With this extraordinary state of mind a strange conception is associated, viz. 2. of certain regions, where besides gods and other beings also such men have their abode, that are growing more and more perfect and are stripping off every personal quality, whether good or bad, v. Köpp. I, 255. — bsam-mnó (bsam mno) or -bló (- blo), also blo-bsám (blo bsam) thinking, wishing etc., bsam-mnó byéd-pa (bsam mno byed pa) or gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba), resp. tŭgs-bsám gtóṅ-ba (thugs bsam gtong ba) Pth., to think, to meditate, consider, think upon frq.; bzáṅ-byed-kyi bsam- (bzang byed kyi bsam-)
